answer:
\boxed{G}

The federal statute is valid because Congress has **exclusive legislative authority** over the District of Columbia under the **U.S. Constitution (Article I, § 8, cl. 17)**, which grants Congress plenary power to govern the federal enclave. The Washington, D.C. City Council lacks jurisdiction over matters where Congress has exclusive authority, including local traffic regulations in the District. This principle overrides any conflicting local ordinance.
